content: I'm feeling really excited about my work right now. I just bought a house in Berlin and I'm in the process of renovating it.
Congratulations on the new house, Riley! That's a big milestone. How's the renovation process going?
It's going well, but it's a lot of work. I've been spending a lot of time researching and planning, and I've also been trying to learn some new skills, like plumbing and electrical work.
That's impressive. It's great that you're taking on the challenge and learning new things. What inspired you to buy a house in Berlin?

I've always loved the city and the culture, and I've been spending a lot of time here for work. I'm a journalist, so I get to travel and cover different stories, but Berlin has always felt like home to me.
That makes sense. It's great that you've found a place that feels like home. What kind of stories have you been covering lately?
I've been covering a lot of tech stories, which is really interesting to me. I've also been getting into mechanical keyboards, which is a bit of a niche hobby, but I really enjoy it.
I've been building my own custom keyboards and learning about the different types of switches and keycaps. It's really fascinating to me.
That's really cool. It's great that you've found a hobby that you're passionate about. Do you have any plans to write about mechanical keyboards or tech in general?
I do, actually. I'm thinking about writing a piece about the intersection of tech and culture, and how it's changing the way we live and work.
That sounds like a fascinating topic. I'm sure you'll have a lot to say about it. Have you considered reaching out to some experts in the field to get their input and insights?
I have, and I've already spoken to a few people. I'm also planning a trip to the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas next month to check out the latest tech trends.
That sounds like a great opportunity. I'm sure you'll learn a lot and have some interesting stories to share.
I'm also thinking about starting a podcast about tech and culture, maybe with a focus on mechanical keyboards and other niche hobbies.
That's a great idea, Riley. You could have some really interesting conversations and interviews with experts and enthusiasts in the field.
